name: Kaitai Struct (Declarative Binary Format Compiler)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT + GPLv3 for compiler); Kaitai Project

auth.notes: Local library; ksc compiler required to generate parsers from .ksy files

performance.notes: Generated parser performance; comparable to hand-written struct parsing

agent_readiness.notes: Binary format compiler from .ksy YAML → Python/C++/Java/JS/etc. Workflow: write format.ksy in YAML. ksc -t python format.ksy → format.py. from kaitaistruct import KaitaiStream, BytesIO. from format import Format. obj = Format(KaitaiStream(BytesIO(data))). obj.magic, obj.width, obj.header.flags. Format library: 350+ pre-built parsers (PNG, ZIP, ELF, PE, JPEG, GIF, MP3, etc.) at https://formats.kaitai.io/. kaitaistruct runtime: pip install kaitaistruct. Lazy evaluation with _io seeking. Nested types. Enums. Instances (computed fields). Switches. Cross-references via _root, _parent.

gotchas: Requires ksc compiler to generate parser — two-step workflow, not pure Python
ksc is a Scala-based compiler — needs JVM installed
Generated parsers are read-only — no build/serialization from .ksy
No type annotations in generated Python code
Web IDE available but ksc CLI setup can be complex on some platforms
best_when: Multi-language binary format parsing (write once, generate C++/Python/Java/JS)
When pre-built format library (PNG, ZIP, ELF, PE) is sufficient
Reverse engineering with WebIDE visualization of bit-level structures
avoid_when: When serialization (build) is needed — use construct instead
Quick one-off Python parsing — struct or construct is faster to set up
Environments without JVM (ksc compiler requires Java)
summary: Kaitai Struct compiles .ksy YAML format specs into parsers for 10+ languages. ksc -t python format.ksy → generated parser. obj = Format(KaitaiStream(BytesIO(data))). 350+ pre-built format parsers (PNG, ZIP, ELF, PE). MIT runtime, GPL compiler. Best for multi-language binary parsing or using pre-built format library. Requires JVM for ksc compiler. Read-only (no serialization). WebIDE for visualization. Use construct when build() is also needed.
